title: Bee Removal in Inwood, Manhattan | $350-$550
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in Inwood, Manhattan. Safe, humane extraction $350-$550. Serving Inwood Hill Park area & all of Upper Manhattan. Call today!
intro: Inwood residents dealing with bee infestations need fast, professional removal services that prioritize safety and efficiency. Best Pest Control NYC offers expert bee removal throughout Inwood, from pre-war apartment buildings near Dyckman Street to homes surrounding Inwood Hill Park. Our licensed technicians provide humane bee extraction and relocation services at competitive rates of $350-$550.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Inwood and Upper Manhattan for years, understanding the unique challenges of this northernmost neighborhood. Our technicians are experienced with both residential and commercial properties, providing same-day service when possible and guaranteed results that keep your family and neighbors safe.
localContext: Inwood's blend of historic apartment complexes, single-family homes, and proximity to natural areas like Inwood Hill Park creates ideal conditions for bee activity, especially during spring and summer months. The neighborhood's tree-lined streets and community gardens attract pollinators, and when bees establish nests in walls, eaves, or chimneys, professional removal becomes essential for resident safety.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to a bee removal call in Inwood?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service for most Inwood bee removal requests. Emergency situations involving aggressive swarms or nests near building entrances receive priority scheduling. We serve all Inwood locations from Dyckman Street to the Henry Hudson Bridge area.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 bee removal service include?
faqs.answer: Our bee removal service includes thorough inspection, safe bee extraction or relocation, nest removal, entry point sealing, and cleanup. The final cost depends on nest location, accessibility, colony size, and whether structural repairs are needed. We provide free estimates before beginning work.

faqs.question: Do you relocate bees or exterminate them?
faqs.answer: Whenever possible, we prioritize humane bee relocation, especially for honeybees which are vital pollinators. We work with local beekeepers to relocate colonies safely. In cases where relocation isn't feasible due to aggressive species or safety concerns, we use targeted removal methods that minimize environmental impact.

faqs.question: Can bees return after removal in Inwood?
faqs.answer: We prevent re-infestation by completely removing the hive, cleaning pheromone traces that attract new swarms, and sealing all entry points. Our service includes follow-up inspection options, and we guarantee our work. Inwood's proximity to green spaces means vigilance is important, but proper exclusion work provides long-term protection.
